message:
test(validation): add Columns layout edge case tests

- Add 8 new Columns validation tests:
  - Narrow width and zero width edge cases
  - Many items row calculation
  - Wide Unicode (CJK) character support
  - Content-based width calculation
  - Expand distribution verification
  - Right alignment within columns
  - Padding application

All 23 Columns tests pass, validating:
- Column width calculation algorithm
- Equal width distribution mode
- Expand mode (fill available width)
- Gutter between columns
- Alignment options
- Edge cases handled gracefully

Closes rich_rust-xbu

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-wfq","title":"Validate Tree Rendering Components","description":"Validate Tree components against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Tree struct - root node and recursive rendering
2. TreeNode struct - label, children, expanded state
3. TreeGuides enum - ASCII, ROUNDED, BOLD, DOUBLE guide styles

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Tree indentation calculation per nesting level
- [ ] TreeGuides character sets match Python Rich exactly
- [ ] Branch vs last-child guide selection logic
- [ ] Nested tree depth handling (deep recursion)
- [ ] Tree label styling propagation
- [ ] Empty tree edge case
- [ ] Single-node tree edge case
- [ ] Wide Unicode labels in tree nodes

TREE GUIDE CHARACTERS (verify exact match):
- ASCII: |, +-, `-
- ROUNDED: │, ├──, └──
- BOLD: ┃, ┣━━, ┗━━
- DOUBLE: ║, ╠══, ╚══

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/tree.rs
- Python rich/tree.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:01.220067263-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:48.318509073-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:33:48.318509073-05:00","close_reason":"Tree validation complete: Fixed ASCII branch guide (+-- instead of |--), added 7 new tests for edge cases (empty root, single node, wide unicode, emoji, all guide styles)","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-wph","title":"Create Benchmark Suite","description":"## Objective
Establish a comprehensive benchmark suite using criterion for measuring performance.

## Background
Before optimizing, we need reliable benchmarks to:
1. Establish baseline performance
2. Measure improvement from optimizations
3. Detect regressions in CI

## Benchmarks to Create

### Color Benchmarks
- [ ] Parse named color (cold cache)
- [ ] Parse named color (warm cache, after LRU implemented)
- [ ] Parse hex color
- [ ] Color downgrade (TrueColor -> Standard)

### Style Benchmarks
- [ ] Style::parse() simple
- [ ] Style::parse() complex
- [ ] Style::combine()
- [ ] Style::render_to_ansi()

### Text Benchmarks
- [ ] Text::render() short text
- [ ] Text::render() long text
- [ ] Text::wrap() narrow width
- [ ] Text::wrap() wide width
- [ ] Text::divide() with many spans

### Table Benchmarks
- [ ] Small table (3x3)
- [ ] Medium table (10x10)
- [ ] Large table (100x100)
- [ ] Table with styled cells
- [ ] Table width calculation only

### Cell Width Benchmarks
- [ ] cell_len() ASCII
- [ ] cell_len() mixed (ASCII + CJK)
- [ ] cell_len() emoji-heavy

### End-to-End Benchmarks
- [ ] Console::print() with markup
- [ ] Panel rendering
- [ ] Full page render (table + panel + text)

## Implementation
```rust
// benches/benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};

fn color_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    c.bench_function("parse_named_color", |b| {
        b.iter(|| Color::parse("red"))
    });
}

criterion_group!(benches, color_parsing);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Files to Create
- benches/benchmarks.rs
- Cargo.toml (add criterion dev-dependency)","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmark suite created. All major code paths covered. Results documented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:55.471565885-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-wph","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:06.83084456-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-x25","title":"Validate Progress Components (ProgressBar, Spinner)","description":"Validate Progress components against RICH_SPEC.md Section 16 (if exists) or Python Rich source.

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. ProgressBar struct and rendering
2. BarStyle enum and customization
3. Spinner struct and animation frames

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] ProgressBar width calculation matches spec
- [ ] ProgressBar percentage display formatting
- [ ] ProgressBar fill characters (complete vs incomplete)
- [ ] BarStyle custom character support
- [ ] Spinner frame cycling logic
- [ ] Spinner Unicode character sets
- [ ] Color/style support for all components

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Progress components render to Segments
- Width should respect console width constraints
- Spinner should be stateless (frame index passed in)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/progress.rs
- Python rich/progress_bar.py for reference
- Python rich/spinner.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:00.317920614-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:25:10.265841141-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:25:10.265841141-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Progress Components

### Key Finding: Phase 2+ Feature Implemented Early

**RICH_SPEC.md explicitly states**: "Progress bars | Separate concern, Phase 2+"

This means Progress components are **NOT in Phase 1 scope** and have no formal spec to validate against. The implementation is ahead of schedule.

---

### ProgressBar Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 197-612)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Width calculation | ✅ | Respects available space |
| Percentage display | ✅ | show_percentage option |
| ETA calculation | ✅ | Based on elapsed time |
| Speed (items/sec) | ✅ | show_speed option |
| Elapsed time | ✅ | show_elapsed option |
| Description | ✅ | Optional task name |
| Finished message | ✅ | Custom completion text |
| Brackets | ✅ | Optional [ ] around bar |
| Style support | ✅ | Separate styles for completed/remaining/pulse |

**BarStyle enum** (5 variants):
- Ascii: # and -
- Block: █ and ░ (default)
- Line: ━ and ─
- Dots: ● and ○
- Gradient: █ and ░ with ▓ pulse

---

### Spinner Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 66-195)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Frame cycling | ✅ | next_frame() advances |
| Stateless query | ✅ | current_frame() doesn't advance |
| Style support | ✅ | Applied to rendered segment |
| Custom frames | ✅ | custom() constructor |

**Preset spinners** (7 variants):
- dots: ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏
- line: ⎺⎻⎼⎽⎼⎻
- simple: |/-\
- bounce: ⠁⠂⠄⠂
- growing: ⣾⣽⣻⢿⡿⣟⣯⣷
- moon: 🌑🌒🌓🌔🌕🌖🌗🌘
- clock: 🕐🕑🕒🕓🕔🕕🕖🕗🕘🕙🕚🕛

---

### Unit Tests ✅ COMPREHENSIVE

20 tests covering:
- ProgressBar creation and updates
- Rendering with various options
- All bar styles
- Spinner frame cycling
- Duration formatting
- Progress clamping (0.0-1.0)

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS

**Status**: Implemented ahead of schedule (Phase 2+ feature)
**Quality**: Well-designed with builder pattern and comprehensive tests
**Recommendation**: No changes needed; ready for use

Note: Since there's no formal spec, validation was done against implementation quality and Python Rich patterns.","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-xbu","title":"Validate Columns Layout Algorithm","description":"Validate Columns layout component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Columns renders multiple renderables side-by-side with automatic width distribution.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Column width calculation algorithm
- [ ] Equal width distribution mode
- [ ] Ratio-based width distribution
- [ ] Expand mode (fill available width)
- [ ] Padding between columns
- [ ] Vertical alignment within columns (top/middle/bottom)
- [ ] Overflow handling when content exceeds column width
- [ ] Empty column handling
- [ ] Single column edge case
- [ ] Mixed-height content alignment

KEY ALGORITHMS:
1. Available width = console_width - (padding * (num_columns - 1))
2. Per-column width = available_width / num_columns (equal mode)
3. Per-column width = available_width * ratio / total_ratio (ratio mode)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/columns.rs
- Python rich/columns.py for reference","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:01.838957632-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:01.838957632-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-xl5","title":"Add Integration Tests for Table Rendering","description":"## Objective
Integration tests verifying complete Table rendering pipeline.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Tables
- [ ] Single row, single column
- [ ] Multiple rows, single column
- [ ] Single row, multiple columns
- [ ] Full table (multiple rows and columns)

### Table Features
- [ ] With title
- [ ] With caption
- [ ] With header row
- [ ] With footer row
- [ ] Row separators (show_lines=true)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)

### Column Sizing
- [ ] Auto-sizing columns
- [ ] Fixed width columns
- [ ] Min/max width constraints
- [ ] Ratio-based expansion
- [ ] Table wider than content (expand=true)
- [ ] Table narrower than content (collapse)

### Cell Features
- [ ] Multi-line cells
- [ ] Styled cell content
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top, middle, bottom)
- [ ] Horizontal alignment (left, center, right)

### Box Styles
- [ ] ASCII box
- [ ] ROUNDED box
- [ ] DOUBLE box
- [ ] HEAVY box
- [ ] safe_box fallback

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table
- [ ] Empty cells
- [ ] Very narrow width
- [ ] Very wide content
- [ ] Wide characters in cells

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/table_integration.rs (new file)","acceptance_criteria":"All table features tested. Rendered output matches expected.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Table integration tests must have DETAILED logging for debugging:

1. **Table structure**: Columns, rows, options
2. **Width calculation**: 
   - Available width
   - Minimum widths per column
   - Maximum widths per column
   - Calculated widths
   - Collapse/expand operations
3. **Row rendering**: Content per cell, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Characters used, positions

This is critical because table layout bugs are hard to diagnose without logs.

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_table_width_calculation() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("table_width_calc", width = 50);
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").width(20))
        .add_column(Column::new("B"))
        .add_column(Column::new("C"));
    
    debug!(columns = ?table.columns, "Table columns");
    
    let widths = table.calculate_widths(50);
    debug!(result_widths = ?widths, "Calculated widths");
    
    let total: usize = widths.iter().sum();
    info!(total_width = total, available = 50, "Width distribution");
    
    assert!(total <= 50, "Total width exceeds available");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:12.280758298-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:04.217224577-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xl5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.821352752-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-xz9","title":"Add Unit Tests for Markup Parser","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/markup/mod.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Parsing
- [ ] Plain text (no markup)
- [ ] Single tag: [bold]text[/]
- [ ] Self-closing style application
- [ ] Named close: [/bold]

### Nested Tags
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/][/]
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/italic][/bold]
- [ ] Deep nesting (3+ levels)
- [ ] Sibling tags

### Style Inheritance
- [ ] Inner tag inherits outer
- [ ] Inner color overrides outer
- [ ] Attributes combine

### Escaping
- [ ] \[ produces [
- [ ] \] produces ]
- [ ] \\ produces \
- [ ] Mixed escaped and tags

### Error Cases
- [ ] Unclosed tag at EOF
- [ ] Unmatched close tag
- [ ] Invalid style in tag
- [ ] Empty tag []
- [ ] Malformed tag [bold
- [ ] No panic on any input

### Complex Cases
- [ ] [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] [bold red on blue]text[/]
- [ ] Multiple tags same line
- [ ] Tags with whitespace

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/markup/mod.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for markup parser. No panics on any input.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Markup parser tests must log the parsing process:

1. **Input**: The markup string being parsed
2. **Tokens**: Each token as it's recognized
3. **Stack**: Tag stack state during parsing
4. **Output**: Final segments with styles

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_nested_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let input = "[bold][red]hello[/red][/bold]";
    info!(input = %input, "Parsing markup");
    
    let segments = render(input);
    
    for (i, seg) in segments.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            text = %seg.text,
            style = ?seg.style,
            "Segment"
        );
    }
    
    assert_eq!(segments.len(), 1);
    let style = segments[0].style.as_ref().unwrap();
    assert!(style.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    
    info!("Nested markup PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:11.457599519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:03.296298648-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xz9","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.769330717-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
